subject: Subject
usthbDetails: Houari Boumediene University of science and technology
m2ssio: Master 2 Systems and services for the Internet of Things
usthb1p1: Three-year academic Bachelor's degree program, organized into semester-based teaching units, focused on the progressive acquisition of theoretical and practical skills in technological sciences and telecommunications.
usthb1p2: This program enabled me to build a strong foundation in mathematics, physics, and electronics before specializing in key areas such as analog and digital telecommunications, networks, and signal processing.
usthb2p1: The first year of the Master's degree in Telecommunications Networks provides advanced knowledge in communication systems, IP networking, signal processing, and modern software and hardware architectures.
usthb2p2: The program combines advanced theoretical coursework with specialized practical labs, strengthening skills in design, modeling, administration, and optimization of telecommunication systems and networks.
usthb2li1: Advanced Digital Communications (MIMO, OFDM, non-ideal channels)
usthb2li2: IP Routing (static and dynamic routing, VLAN, OSPF, EIGRP)
usthb2li4: Advanced Signal Processing (stochastic processes, adaptive filtering)
usthb2li9: Cisco platforms and professional networking environments
ubop1: The TRC Master's degree at the University of Western Brittany is a specialized program in Information Systems Cybersecurity, built on three core areas: Computer Science, Networks, and Telecommunications. Over 450 hours are dedicated to cybersecurity throughout the two-year program.
ubop2: Highly selective (20 students per year) and available as a work-study program, this Master's degree combines advanced theoretical coursework and hands-on training (labs, mini-projects, long-term projects, and a 6-month internship), enabling immediate professional integration or PhD continuation.
uboli1: Data compression and error-correcting coding (voice, data, video)
uboli2: Digital transmission standards and radio access technologies (2G, 3G, 4G, 5G)
uboli3: Design, sizing, and administration of network architectures
uboli5: Voice over IP (VoIP) and Quality of Service (QoS)
uboli11: Risk analysis (ISO 2700X) and secure software lifecycle
uboli12: Attack detection, incident response, and defense strategies
upemp1: The Master’s degree in Systems and Services for the Internet of Things (SSIoT) focuses on the design, implementation, and evaluation of IoT solutions integrating communication systems, large-scale data processing, security, and distributed architectures.
upemp2: The program covers the entire IoT stack: sensors, constrained networks, cloud computing, DevOps, cybersecurity, and artificial intelligence. It includes a long-term internship or work-study track, enabling strong professional integration or PhD continuation.
upemli1: Data collection, transport, and routing for IoT systems
upemli2: Wireless and cellular technologies for IoT
upemli3: IoT systems, data, and network security
upemli6: Web services and IoT communication protocols
upemli8: Explainable AI and data flow Quality of Service (QoS)
upemli9: IoT hardware architecture and sensing systems
upemli10: Project management, professional communication, and final internship
algerieTelecomParagraph: As an intern for my final year project, I conducted work focused on computer network security, including simulating attacks such as Man-In-The-Middle, Brute Force, and Phishing. This project allowed me to analyze system vulnerabilities, understand the exploitation mechanisms used by attackers, and propose concrete solutions to strengthen the protection of network infrastructures.
algerieTelecomLi1: Simulation of network attacks: Man-in-the-Middle (MITM), Brute Force, and Phishing in a controlled environment.
algerieTelecomLi2: Analysis of infrastructure vulnerabilities and identification of exploited security weaknesses.
algerieTelecomLi3: Implementation of countermeasures: communication encryption, strong password policies, and phishing awareness.
algerieTelecomLi4: Configuration and security hardening of network equipment (firewalls, routers, secure protocols).
algerieTelecomLi5: Preparation of a detailed technical report describing attack scenarios, impacts, and security recommendations.
algerieTelecomLi6: Testing and validation of implemented solutions to assess their effectiveness.
nfgParagraph: As a freestyle performer and event host, I collaborated with numerous national and international brands such as Pepsi, Coca-Cola, Ramy, Candia, Lactel, Alfa Romeo, Samsung, and Acer, as well as the Italian Embassy in Algiers. I took part in promotional campaigns, product launches, institutional events, and charity initiatives dedicated to sick children. I also participated in a major nationwide summer tour across Algeria with the brand Lesieur. These experiences allowed me to develop strong stage presence, adaptability, and the ability to effectively engage audiences in alignment with each brand’s identity and objectives.
nfgLi1: Freestyle performance and event hosting for promotional and institutional events.
nfgLi2: Collaboration with national and international brands: Pepsi, Coca-Cola, Ramy, Candia, Lactel, Alfa Romeo, Samsung, Acer, as well as the Italian Embassy in Algiers.
nfgLi3: Participation in charity initiatives and association events dedicated to sick children.
nfgLi4: Artistic representation during product launches, marketing campaigns, and public events.
nfgLi5: Participation in a major nationwide summer tour across Algeria with the brand Lesieur.
nfgLi6: Audience engagement and performance adaptation according to brand identity and event context.
capgeminiParagraph: As part of my Master’s degree in IoT, I completed a final-year project called "Voisin Malin", which consisted of designing and developing from scratch an application used by field investigators to conduct door-to-door surveys. The application was required to operate in both online and offline modes, with automatic data synchronization once network connectivity was restored — a feature I personally designed and implemented. The project was carried out using Agile methodology and deployed on AWS Cloud.
capgeminiLi1: Project management using Agile methodology (Scrum), with use of monitoring tools (Jira) and versioning (Git).
capgeminiLi2: Development of the persistence layer (data management and database integration).
capgeminiLi3: Implementation of the service layer (business logic and APIs).
capgeminiLi4: Development of user interfaces (UI) using Angular 7.
capgeminiLi5: Use of Node.js 10 and AWS Amplify for backend and serverless architecture.
capgeminiLi6: Deployment and configuration of AWS Cloud services (Amplify Serverless, Cognito, Aurora SQL, CloudFront).
capgeminiLi7: Implementation of offline mode with automatic data synchronization when network connectivity is restored.
viverisParagraph1: At Viveris, a digital services company, I worked for approximately four months on a project for ICF Habitat, the real estate subsidiary of SNCF. The objective was to develop a property management application enabling tenants to access rent receipts, invoices, administrative documents, support requests, and other online services. As the application was already advanced when I joined, I mainly focused on accessibility improvements and theme management. I organized and led workshops with visually impaired and blind users to ensure compliance with accessibility standards and deliver an inclusive user experience.
viverisParagraph2: Following this project, I joined a team working on a multi-product insurance platform, where I am currently assigned. My responsibilities include maintenance, performance optimization, and feature enhancements for auto, home, and agricultural insurance products, as well as the development of new products such as professional multi-risk insurance and health protection (currently in progress). I am also involved in Angular migrations, creation of dedicated Angular libraries per product, Azure CI/CD pipeline management, technical documentation, and close collaboration with business teams to refine detailed functional specifications (DFS). In addition, I actively contribute internally through technical interviews, creation of recruitment technical tests, Angular tech talks, and obtaining mid-level and senior Angular certifications.
viverisLi1: Development of a property management application for ICF Habitat (SNCF subsidiary).
viverisLi2: Implementation and enhancement of accessibility features (workshops with visually impaired and blind users).
viverisLi4: Corrective and evolutionary maintenance of a multi-product insurance platform.
viverisLi6: Feature enhancements for auto, home, and agricultural insurance products.
viverisLi7: Design and development of new insurance products: professional multi-risk and health protection.
viverisLi9: Creation of dedicated Angular libraries per product for standardization and reuse.
viverisLi10: Management and optimization of Azure DevOps CI/CD pipelines.
viverisLi11: Technical documentation and collaboration with business stakeholders to refine detailed functional specifications.
viverisLi12: Internal contributions: technical interviews, recruitment test design, Angular certifications (mid-level and senior), and Angular tech talks.
